heroTitle: Storefront composition is now real, while the home page stays modular.
heroDescription: The shell, CMS, catalog, cart, and checkout slices are now live against Darwin.WebApi. Home stays intentionally light, but it is no longer a single blank-state block.
heroHighlightTheme: Theme tokens and shell chrome stay outside feature slices.
heroHighlightComposition: Home sections are composed as explicit web parts instead of a one-off layout.
metricsTitle: Current public delivery is measurable, not a hidden starter shell.
metricsDescription: These numbers come from the same live public CMS/catalog contracts and runtime configuration that the storefront currently uses.
metricPagesNote: total items currently visible through the public CMS list
metricPagesDegradedNote: public CMS list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricProductsNote: total items currently visible through the public catalog list
metricProductsDegradedNote: public catalog list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricCategoriesNote: public category entries currently available for browsing
metricCategoriesDegradedNote: public category list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricCulturesNote: active front-office cultures currently configured at runtime
discoveryReadinessTitle: Home should show how ready CMS and catalog are for live browse.
discoveryReadinessDescription: This section keeps public discovery debt visible from the storefront entry route, so CMS and catalog browse quality is easier to read before the visitor drills into those surfaces.
discoveryReadinessCmsDescription: {readyCount} visible pages are discovery-ready while {attentionCount} still need metadata attention.
discoveryReadinessCatalogDescription: {offerCount} visible offer products, {baseCount} visible base products, and {categoryCount} visible category lanes currently support browse.
discoveryReadinessEmptyMessage: Browse readiness should stay visible on Home even if one public discovery surface degrades.
priorityTitle: Home should surface the most actionable live next steps.
priorityDescription: This section ranks the current storefront and member follow-up paths from live cart, billing, loyalty, order, CMS, and catalog signals instead of making the visitor assemble the next move from separate route teasers.
priorityCheckoutTitle: Continue checkout from the live cart
priorityCheckoutDescription: {itemCount} live cart lines are already in progress. Current gross total: {total}.
priorityLoyaltyDescription: Current next reward: {reward}. Remaining points to the next reward: {points}.
priorityProductDescription: The current product spotlight is live at {price}.
priorityPageFallbackDescription: Continue into the current published CMS spotlight from the same public content contract.
priorityEmptyMessage: Home should still keep route-map and recovery sections visible even if no priority lane item is currently derivable.
commerceOpportunityTitle: Home should show the strongest live buying move, not only generic entry points.
commerceOpportunityDescription: This section turns live cart, spotlight product, and category-lane signals into a small commercial action board so the storefront entry can push the shopper toward the highest-value next move.
commerceOpportunityCartTitle: A live basket is already strong enough to continue into checkout.
commerceOpportunityCartDescription: {itemCount} live cart lines are already in progress. Current gross total: {total}.
commerceOpportunityProductTitle: {product} is currently the clearest live product follow-up.
commerceOpportunityProductDescription: Current spotlight price: {price}. Use this route when the shopper should move directly from Home into a concrete product decision.
commerceOpportunityCategoryTitle: {category} is currently the strongest browse lane.
commerceOpportunityCategoryDescription: This category already exposes a live storefront follow-up through {product}, so Home can move the shopper into browse with a concrete commercial angle.
commerceOpportunityCategoryFallbackDescr: This category lane is visible, but the category-specific product follow-up is currently degraded with status {status}.
commerceOpportunityEmptyMessage: Home should keep other route and recovery parts visible even when no live commerce opportunity can currently be derived.
offerBoardTitle: Home should expose multiple strong buying opportunities, not only one spotlight.
offerBoardDescription: This section ranks the currently visible catalog opportunities by the strongest savings signal so the storefront entry can show a small live offer board instead of a single product suggestion.
offerBoardCartAwareDescription: This section now shifts away from {count} currently cart-linked product routes so storefront entry can suggest the next buying move beyond the active shopping flow instead of echoing the same basket.
offerBoardOfferDescription: Current savings signal: {savings}% against the visible compare-at price. Current storefront price: {price}.
offerBoardOfferFallbackDescription: This product is currently visible without a stronger compare-at signal. Current storefront price: {price}.
offerBoardEmptyMessage: Home should keep the rest of the storefront composition visible even when no live offers can currently be derived.
campaignBoardTitle: Home should also sell through live category stories, not only individual products.
campaignBoardDescription: This board turns the strongest currently visible category lanes into campaign-style entry points so the storefront can move the shopper into browse with a clearer commercial angle.
campaignBoardProductDescription: {product} currently anchors this category with a visible saving signal of {savings}% and a live price of {price}.
campaignBoardFallbackProductDescription: {product} currently anchors this category at a live price of {price}.
campaignBoardCategoryFallbackDescription: This category is currently visible, but its featured product follow-up is degraded with status {status}.
campaignBoardProductMetaFallback: No stronger compare-at anchor is currently visible.
campaignBoardEmptyMessage: Home should keep the rest of the storefront composition visible even if no live category campaigns can currently be derived.
routeMapTitle: Home should expose the next real route, not only section teasers.
routeMapDescription: This web part keeps the current public browse and follow-up routes explicit, including the next concrete detail or member destination the visitor can actually open right now.
routeMapCmsTitle: Start from the published content index and continue into a live page.
routeMapCmsHealthyDescription: The content route is healthy enough to expose both the CMS index and a concrete published page detail from the same public contract family.
routeMapCmsDegradedDescription: The CMS route is currently degraded with status {status}, so Home keeps both the browse entry and a safe follow-up path explicit instead of hiding the content layer.
routeMapCmsFallbackMeta: No published spotlight page is currently available; account entry stays visible as the safe next move.
routeMapCatalogTitle: Move from catalog browse into a real product detail route.
routeMapCatalogHealthyDescription: The browse route is healthy enough to expose both the catalog index and a concrete product detail path from the same public storefront contracts.
routeMapCatalogDegradedDescription: Catalog continuity currently depends on product status {productsStatus} and category status {categoriesStatus}, so Home keeps browse plus a safe next move explicit.
routeMapCatalogFallbackMeta: No spotlight product is currently available; checkout remains the safe next storefront move.
routeMapAccountTitle: Keep self-service and loyalty visible as the next front-office layer.
routeMapAccountDescription: Account, sign-in, loyalty, orders, and invoices should stay connected as the follow-up layer of the same storefront system rather than a detached portal jump.
routeMapAccountMeta: Member follow-up already lives on the shared front-office shell.
routeMapEmptyMessage: The route map is app-composed and should stay available even if one live surface temporarily degrades.
memberResumeTitle: Continue the signed-in front-office journey for {email}.
memberResumeDescription: A member session is currently active until {expiry}. Home should expose direct re-entry into the protected portal instead of behaving like a purely anonymous landing page.
memberResumeAccountDescription: Return to the member landing route that now combines profile, commerce, and loyalty follow-up.
memberResumeAccountMeta: Portal landing and action-center are already live.
memberResumeOrdersDescription: Jump back into post-purchase history and billing follow-up without leaving the shared front-office shell.
memberResumeOrdersMeta: Orders and invoices already run on the protected portal routes.
memberResumeLoyaltyDescription: Go back into joined-business dashboards, reward focus, and loyalty discovery from the same signed-in storefront session.
memberResumeLoyaltyMeta: Loyalty overview, discovery, and business detail already share the member shell.
memberResumeRecentOrderDescription: Recent storefront order from {createdAt}. Current total: {total}.
memberResumeInvoiceDescription: Current invoice status: {status}. Outstanding balance or current total in view: {balance}.
memberResumeCheckoutDescription: A reusable member address is already available for checkout in {city}, {countryCode}.
memberResumeCheckoutAddressTitle: Prepare member address book for checkout
memberResumeCheckoutAddressDescription: Member address status: {status}. Add a reusable address so checkout can start from canonical member data.
memberResumeLoyaltyFocusDescription: {points} points remain until the next reward. Current next reward: {reward}.
memberResumeEmptyMessage: Signed-in member resume routes should stay available when a member session exists.
cartResumeTitle: Continue the storefront flow you already started.
cartResumeDescription: {count} recent cart-linked product snapshots are currently available from browser-owned storefront state, so Home can offer a direct path back into cart and checkout.
cartResumeItemDescription: Resume this storefront item from the browser-owned cart snapshot. SKU: {sku}.
cartResumeItemFallbackDescription: Resume this storefront item from the browser-owned cart snapshot.
cartResumeCartDescription: Open the current cart route and continue quantity, coupon, and next-step review from the same storefront session.
cartResumeCartMeta: Cart route already shares the storefront shell and continuity rails.
cartResumeCheckoutDescription: Jump back into checkout if the shopper is already moving toward shipping, payment, and confirmation.
cartResumeCheckoutMeta: Checkout already reuses cart state, saved addresses, and readiness signals.
cartResumeEmptyMessage: Cart resume routes should stay hidden unless browser-owned storefront state already exists.
cartWindowTitle: Live cart totals should stay visible on the storefront entry route.
cartWindowDescription: Current cart status: {status}. {itemCount} cart lines are currently available through the live public cart contract.
cartWindowCartDescription: {itemCount} cart lines are currently active and ready for quantity, coupon, and continuation review.
cartWindowCheckoutDescription: Open checkout and continue from the same live cart summary instead of rebuilding the purchase flow.
cartWindowCheckoutCouponDescription: The current cart already carries coupon code {coupon}. Open checkout and continue from the same live cart summary.
cartWindowEmptyMessage: Cart summary should stay hidden unless a live public cart already exists.
contractRailTitle: Public routes should expose contract health, not hide it.
contractRailDescription: This web part keeps the current CMS, catalog, and account-entry surfaces visible as actionable storefront lanes instead of leaving runtime state buried in one hero panel.
contractCmsHealthyDescription: The public CMS list is responding cleanly enough to drive navigation, spotlight sections, and public content browsing from the same published source.
contractCmsDegradedDescription: The public CMS list is currently degraded with status {status}, so storefront content should stay observable and actionable instead of silently collapsing.
contractCmsMeta: {count} pages currently loaded into the home spotlight.
contractCatalogHealthyDescription: The public catalog list is healthy enough to drive browse, product detail, cart follow-up, and home spotlight reuse from the same contract family.
contractCatalogDegradedDescription: The public catalog list is currently degraded with status {status}, so browse and merchandising follow-up should remain visible instead of pretending discovery is complete.
contractCatalogMeta: {count} products currently loaded into the home spotlight.
contractAccountDescription: Registration, sign-in, and member follow-up already live on the same shell, so front-office account entry should stay part of one system rather than a detached auth island.
contractAccountMeta: {count} runtime cultures currently supported by the front-office shell.
contractRailEmptyMessage: The contract rail is app-composed and should stay available even when individual public feeds degrade.
stageFlowTitle: The storefront should read like one staged customer journey.
stageFlowDescription: This web part keeps content, discovery, and follow-up in one deliberate sequence so Home composition communicates system flow rather than only isolated feature entry points.
stageContentDescription: The CMS surface should frame what the shopper is looking at before product discovery begins, using the same published content truth that operators manage.
stageDiscoveryTitle: Move into browse and product selection
stageDiscoveryDescription: Catalog browsing should pick up directly from that context and keep the shopper inside the same storefront language, contract set, and shell.
stageFollowUpTitle: Keep account and member follow-up connected
stageFollowUpDescription: Account entry, loyalty, orders, and invoices should remain follow-up surfaces of the same storefront system instead of a separate sub-application.
stageFollowUpMeta: Member follow-up already lives on the shared front-office shell.
stageFlowEmptyMessage: The stage-flow composition is app-owned and should stay available even when one public data source degrades.
pairPanelTitle: Content and discovery should stay in dialogue.
pairPanelDescription: This web part keeps CMS and catalog side by side so storefront storytelling and product discovery can be compared as two active surfaces of the same system.
pairCmsHealthyDescription: The storefront can already frame the public experience with published CMS pages, navigation, and long-form detail routes from the same contract family.
pairCmsDegradedDescription: The CMS side is currently degraded with status {status}, so the storefront should keep that weakness visible instead of pretending the narrative layer is complete.
pairCmsMeta: {count} CMS pages are currently loaded into the home spotlight.
pairCatalogHealthyDescription: The public catalog is already strong enough to carry category browse, product detail, cart follow-up, and merchandising reuse from the same data source.
pairCatalogDegradedDescription: The catalog side is currently degraded with status {status}, so discovery follow-up should stay explicit rather than hiding behind generic storefront chrome.
pairCatalogMeta: {count} catalog products are currently loaded into the home spotlight.
agendaTitle: Home should keep the main storefront streams visible at once.
agendaDescription: This web part keeps content, commerce, and member follow-up side by side so the front-office can show multiple active streams without collapsing everything into one narrative mode.
agendaContentDescription: CMS should keep the storefront grounded in published narrative context and support pages, not only in product cards.
agendaContentBulletOne: Published CMS index and detail routes already run on the live public contract.
agendaContentMeta: {count} CMS items are currently featured on Home.
agendaCommerceDescription: Catalog, cart, checkout, and confirmation now form a real storefront commerce path and should stay visible as one active stream.
agendaCommerceBulletOne: Catalog and storefront commerce already share locale-aware routing and live public contracts.
agendaCommerceMeta: {count} catalog items are currently featured on Home.
agendaMemberDescription: Account entry, loyalty, orders, and invoices should remain visible as follow-up surfaces of the same storefront system.
agendaMemberBulletOne: Self-service, sign-in, and member portal routes already share the front-office shell.
agendaMemberBulletTwo: Localization and route hygiene are already unified across public and member surfaces.
agendaMemberMeta: {count} runtime cultures currently support the shared shell.
agendaEmptyMessage: The agenda-columns composition is app-owned and should remain available even if one stream degrades.
shortcutsTitle: The current public surface is already navigable end to end.
shortcutsDescription: These shortcuts represent the currently implemented foundation before richer merchandising or campaign composition is added.
shortcutCmsDescription: Open the public CMS index and validate the same published page truth that WebAdmin now manages.
shortcutCatalogDescription: Browse published categories and products directly from Darwin.WebApi without relying on starter placeholders.
shortcutAccountDescription: Registration, activation, and password-reset self-service can grow here before full authenticated member sessions arrive.
shortcutsEmptyMessage: Storefront shortcuts are app-defined and should always be available.
journeysTitle: Each public route should already behave like part of one system.
journeysDescription: These follow-up paths keep CMS, catalog, and account entry points visible with their current contract state instead of scattering those routes across unrelated placeholders.
journeyCmsDescription: Move from the home surface into the public CMS index and then into the published page detail routes managed by the same backend contract.
journeyCatalogDescription: Move directly into the public catalog list and keep the same culture-aware catalog contracts through listing and detail pages.
journeyAccountDescription: Enter registration, sign-in, and self-service/member follow-up from the same shell instead of treating auth as a separate mini-app.
journeyAccountMeta: Self-service and member portal are already on the live front-office shell.
journeysEmptyMessage: These route follow-ups are part of the application shell and should remain available.
recoveryRailTitle: Public entry should still offer a clear next move when one surface is degraded.
recoveryRailDescription: This rail keeps CMS, catalog, and account recovery paths explicit so Home can guide the visitor through the current public system even when one contract family is partially degraded.
recoveryCmsHealthyDescription: CMS is healthy enough to keep the public narrative layer and support pages available as the first recovery surface.
recoveryCmsDegradedDescription: CMS is currently degraded with status {status}, but the public content route should still stay visible as a recovery path.
recoveryCmsMeta: {count} CMS spotlight items are currently available on Home.
recoveryCatalogTitle: Recover through browse and product discovery
recoveryCatalogHealthyDescription: Catalog and category contracts are healthy enough to keep storefront browse and product follow-up available as the main recovery path.
recoveryCatalogDegradedDescription: Catalog recovery currently depends on product status {productsStatus} and category status {categoriesStatus}; the route should stay explicit instead of disappearing behind generic chrome.
recoveryCatalogMeta: {count} catalog spotlight items are currently available on Home.
recoveryAccountTitle: Recover through account and member entry
recoveryAccountDescription: If public content or discovery is weak, the storefront should still keep account, self-service, and member follow-up visible as part of the same front-office system.
recoveryAccountMeta: Self-service and member entry remain available on the shared shell.
recoveryRailEmptyMessage: Recovery rails are app-composed and should remain visible even if one public feed degrades.
cmsSpotlightTitle: Published pages are now available for storefront composition.
cmsSpotlightDescription: This section is fed from the public CMS page list contract, not from a hard-coded marketing stub.
cmsPageDescriptionFallback: Published storefront content available through the public CMS contract.
cmsSpotlightEmptyMessage: No published CMS pages are available for the current home spotlight.
productSpotlightTitle: Representative public products can now be surfaced on home.
productSpotlightDescription: This section deliberately uses the current public catalog contract so later merchandising can extend from real data instead of a fake hero grid.
productDescriptionFallback: Published product card delivered from the public catalog surface.
productSpotlightEmptyMessage: No published products are currently available for the home spotlight.
categorySpotlightTitle: Published categories should lead directly into live storefront discovery.
categorySpotlightDescription: This section reuses the current public category and product contracts to show where each browse lane can already take the shopper right now.
categorySpotlightCardDescription: Move from this category straight into live catalog discovery. Current spotlight product: {productName}.
categorySpotlightFallbackDescription: This category is visible, but the category-specific product lane is currently degraded with status {status}.
categorySpotlightEmptyMessage: No published categories are currently available for the home category spotlight.
